title: ctenitis magna
intro: Ctenitis magna is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of the Pacific Islands. The leaves of the Ctenitis magna plant are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which are said to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and acting as a natural antiseptic to prevent infection. Ctenitis magna is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
